name: Automated Sentinel
oracle_text:
Malfunction 3 (This permanent enters tapped with three malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.).
hexproof, malfunction
When ~ no longer has malfunction counters on it, draw a card.
flavor_text: Three centuries of dormancy in the vaults beneath Mount Rendon. When power surged back through its crystalline matrices, it remembered only one directive: observe.

validation_errors: Line 1: "This permanent" should use "~" or "this" only in specific MTG contexts
Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(This permanent enters tapped with three malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)"
Malfunction already causes the permanent to enter tapped — explicit 'enters tapped' is redundant
Combined oracle + flavor text is 441 characters, exceeding the 350-char limit for this card type

design_notes: This is a Blue rare artifact creature with a high malfunction threshold (3 counters). The card is designed as a strong payoff for the Malfunction mechanic: hexproof while in-flight provides protection during the vulnerable ramp-up phase, and drawing a card when fully online rewards players for keeping it alive. The 3/3 body for CMC 2 is above rate, justified by: (1) three turns of delay before the card becomes untapped and threatening, (2) vulnerability to artifact removal during that window, (3) hexproof creating counterplay restrictions. This fits Blue's defensive, spell-slinging archetype and encourages artifact-focused control/midrange strategies in the U/W (Ancient Technology) or U/R (Mad Science) archetypes. The ETB draw triggers once when all counters are gone, providing a one-time card advantage payoff rather than sustained value, keeping it balanced for rare.
